attacking_style: Opportunistic rather than planned; attacks on short climbs within 5km of finish or exploits disorganized sprint finales. Bridges to late breakaways solo rather than initiating attacks from peloton.
team_tactics: Heavily reliant on BORA team structure for positioning and intermediate sprint setups. When team focuses elsewhere (GC ambitions) or absent, struggles with sprint positioning and wastes energy finding wheels. Team rides sustained tempo to shed pure sprinters on selective stages.
terrain_preference: Selective hilly sprint finishes where climbing pace eliminates pure sprinters. Technical, chaotic finales where positioning judgment matters. Historically strong on cobbles but decline evident 2021+. Struggles on sustained hilly terrain 15km+.
vulnerability: Increasingly crash-prone 2021-2023 (crashed early Paris-Roubaix 2021 and 2023, multiple Tour crashes). Exposed when team unavailable for leadout/positioning. Dropped unexpectedly on longer hilly approaches despite team pacing. Declining bike handling on cobbled classics.
key_patterns: Proactive green jersey tactics: attacks/contests intermediate sprints then abandons breakaways, purely mathematical play (2019 Tour repeated)
Wins by exploiting chaos: disintegrated leadout trains, solo bridges to late attacks, technical finishes without formal sprint (4+ instances)
Team-dependent positioning: multiple observations show energy waste and vulnerability without BORA leadout structure in bunch sprints
Climbing ability separates from pure sprinters: survives selective pace to win reduced sprints, unusual uphill power for sprinter category
Crash pattern emerging 2021-2023: early crashes Paris-Roubaix twice, Tour finale crash, declining cobbled handling noted explicitly
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 shows reasonable sprint legs but continued crash problems (Flanders, Roubaix early exits). Nine-month winless streak before 2022 Tour de Suisse win indicates inconsistency. Declining cobbled classics strength explicitly noted.
